causal_chain.mechanism: CRISPR's guide RNA directs Cas enzyme to a specific DNA sequence, where Cas makes a cut, allowing for gene editing or disruption.
Modifying a disease-causing gene can correct the genetic defect or prevent its expression, thus preventing the disease.

confounders: Other genetic factors influencing disease susceptibility
missing_links: Efficacy of gene modification in preventing disease manifestation (i.e., does the modification actually prevent the disease in all cases?)
Long-term effects of gene modification (are there unforeseen consequences?)
Delivery method of CRISPR to target cells (does the delivery method affect the outcome?)
